description:
You are a marine safety investigator at the UK MAIB examining a collision between two vessels in the Solent approaches. Evidence from Voyage Data Recorders and VTS radar is at /home/ga/Documents/Investigation/ (vessel_a_vdr.csv, vessel_b_vdr.csv, vts_traffic_snapshot.csv, weather_conditions.txt).

Extrapolate Ships A and B's VDR tracks to determine the collision point coordinates, collision time, and COLREGS encounter classification (head-on, crossing, or overtaking). Identify the give-way vessel. Compute CPA and TCPA for all vessel pairs based on their recorded tracks. Design a COLREGS-compliant avoidance maneuver for the give-way vessel achieving CPA >= 1.0nm from the collision partner AND CPA >= 0.5nm from all other traffic simultaneously, using the minimum course alteration that satisfies all constraints.

Create a reconstruction scenario at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/n) Collision Reconstruction/ reproducing all four vessels' tracks from the evidence using waypoint legs. Create an avoidance scenario at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/o) Avoidance Demonstration/ identical to the reconstruction except with the give-way vessel's altered course.

Write a formal investigation report at /home/ga/Documents/Investigation/collision_report.txt covering collision coordinates and time, encounter classification citing COLREGS rules, all CPA/TCPA values, the avoidance maneuver details, and achieved CPAs.

Update bc5.ini with full_radar=1, arpa_on=1, max_radar_range=24.

Login: ga / password123
